puzzle.title: The Builders of Towers
puzzle.intro: In a time long past, a mighty civilization rose, distinguished by its impressive feats of construction and formidable strength. This collective entity was granted abundant resources and physical prowess, yet they chose a path of defiance against the divine message sent to guide them. Their story serves as a profound lesson in the annals of history, a testament to the consequences of rejecting truth.
clues: We were granted immense physical strength and stature, unlike any other people of our time.
We were known for building grand structures and lofty pillars in every high place, thinking we would live forever.
A messenger from among us was sent to warn us, calling us to worship Allah alone and to abandon our idols.
We arrogantly questioned our messenger, asking if they came to us only to make us worship Allah and abandon what our ancestors worshipped.
Our land was blessed with gardens, springs, and fertile fields, but we attributed our prosperity to our own might, not to our Creator.
For our persistent disbelief and mockery, we were ultimately annihilated by a terrifying, furious wind that raged for many days and nights.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Being asked to sacrifice a specific camel
Being challenged to produce ten surahs like the Quran
Being shown the splitting of the sea
Being warned by a prophet about the impending flood
Rejecting a prophet who warned against building excessively and worshipping idols
trial.answer: Rejecting a prophet who warned against building excessively and worshipping idols

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Annihilated by a furious, devastating wind
outcome.answer: Annihilated by a furious, devastating wind

puzzle.arabic: فَأَمَّا عَادٌۭ فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ وَقَالُوا۟ مَنْ أَشَدُّ مِنَّا قُوَّةً ۖ أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَهُمْ هُوَ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُمْ قُوَّةًۭ ۖ وَكَانُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ
puzzle.verse: As for ʿAad, they were arrogant upon the earth without right and said, "Who is greater than us in strength?" Did they not consider that Allāh who created them was greater than them in strength? But they were rejecting Our signs. (41:15)
